summary: P1/P2 enhancements fully validated. 28/28 new tests + 39/39 regression tests pass (67/67 total). Covers slowapi rate limiting on /auth/{login,register,forgot-password,reset-password} verified against localhost (real 429s after threshold), 2FA TOTP full lifecycle (setup -> enable with password+code, login block (2fa_required), login w/ invalid code (2fa_invalid), login w/ valid code, /auth/me totp_enabled flag, disable with code, wrong-password/wrong-code/no-setup rejections), X-Total-Count + Access-Control-Expose-Headers on /listings /orders /wallet/withdrawals /notifications /admin/sellers /admin/activity-logs, /admin/analytics (days=7 -> 8 entries, days=14 -> 15 entries, days=30 -> 31 entries, status_breakdown + top_categories + active_sellers_count, seller forbidden), lifespan-based startup with no on_event deprecation warnings in backend logs, admin seeded, storage initialized.

minor.issue: X-Total-Count reflects pre-availability count (computed before the post-filter for `availability`). With availability filter set, total can exceed returned count even when no further pages exist. Acceptable for MVP but UI consumers may show wrong totals when filtering by availability.

No password re-confirmation before generating a pending_secret. Anyone with a valid access token (e.g., stolen) can reset the pending secret. Enable still requires password, so impact is limited, but adding password re-auth on /setup hardens defense-in-depth.
If totp is already disabled the endpoint returns 200 silently regardless of supplied password. Could allow a probe to confirm 2FA-disabled state without auth checks beyond bearer token. Low risk.

totp_code is verified via TOTP.verify(valid_window=1) which allows current and adjacent windows; cool but does NOT prevent replay of the same code within the same 30s window. Consider one-time use tracking for stronger guarantees.

action_items: Optional: re-prompt password in /auth/2fa/setup so a stolen access token cannot rotate the pending TOTP secret without confirming password.
Optional: track used TOTP codes within current 30s window to prevent code replay.
Optional: make /admin/sellers X-Total-Count reflect the actual filtered count (move availability filter into the mongo query rather than post-filter loop).
Document slowapi tuning (10/min login, 5/min register, 3/min forgot, 5/min reset) in README and consider per-user (not only per-IP) keys behind shared CDN.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~1440 lines; should be split into routers (auth, 2fa, listings, orders, wallet, disputes, admin, ws) for maintainability.
Rate limit key_func uses get_remote_address which under a CDN/proxy will see the proxy IP; consider Forwarded/X-Forwarded-For aware key with caution.
/admin/analytics performs an in-Python loop over completed orders to compute top categories; for large datasets a $lookup aggregation would be more efficient.
/admin/sellers iterates users and post-filters by availability AFTER total count is calculated -> total can mismatch page contents when availability filter is active.
2FA pending_secret is stored in plaintext on the user doc - acceptable for MVP, but encrypting at rest (or using separate collection w/ TTL) would be more secure.
InsecureKeyLengthWarning still emitted by PyJWT - JWT_SECRET in backend/.env is shorter than the recommended 32 bytes. Rotate to >=32 chars for prod.
lifespan migration done correctly; no on_event deprecation warnings in startup logs.
test_credentials: admin@sellerhub.io / Admin@12345 (seeded). Test sellers registered dynamically with TEST_p2_<hex>@test.io / Seller@12345.
seed_data_creation: Created 2 dynamic seller users (TEST_p2_*) used for 2FA + RBAC tests. One had 2FA enabled then disabled in teardown. Also created several throwaway TEST_rl_* users via rate-limit test (first 5 succeed, rest 429). No persistent listings/orders/withdrawals created by this iteration.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Rate-limit tests sleep ~62s between classes to reset the slowapi window and hit localhost:8001 directly (Cloudflare rotates client IPs on the public URL). 2FA tests use pyotp.TOTP(secret).now(); always run the disable test or the seller user will be permanently 2FA-blocked. The fixture in Test2FA has teardown that disables 2FA on failure too. Pagination tests assert both X-Total-Count and Access-Control-Expose-Headers: X-Total-Count - frontend must read header through this CORS exposure. Backend lifespan is implemented via @asynccontextmanager and no FastAPI on_event deprecation warnings are emitted. PyJWT still warns InsecureKeyLengthWarning - cosmetic but worth raising JWT_SECRET to >=32 chars.
